title: An improved method for measuring metaldehyde in surface water using liquid chromatography tandem mass spectrometry
author: Schumacher, Castle, et al.

steps_raw.title: Sample preparation
steps_raw.instruction: Dilute stock metaldehyde solution (50 μg/L) in ultrapure water to obtain working calibration standard solutions of concentration 0, 10, 50, 100, 300, 500, 750 and 1000 ng/L

steps_raw.title: Sample preparation
steps_raw.instruction: Attach an Oasis HLB cartridge to the 12 port/6-position selection valve.

steps_raw.title: Sample preparation
steps_raw.instruction: Add 1 mL of sample and working calibration standard solutions into labelled silanized auto-sampler vials. Add 20 μL of internal standard solution (50 μg/L) to each vial and mix. Transfer to auto-sampler tray.

steps_raw.title: Sample preparation
steps_raw.instruction: Load method which contains the entire LC conditions and switching valve programme, as shown in Table 3, via the Mass Hunter data acquisition software.

steps_raw.title: Sample preparation
steps_raw.instruction: Allow system to stabilise for 30 min prior to commencing analysis. Ensure that the on-line enrichment and HPLC system is leak free.

steps_raw.title: Sample preparation
steps_raw.instruction: Analyse samples and calibration standards solutions and prepare a calibration Table via the Mass Hunter data analysis software.

materials: Acetonitrile and methanol of LC–MS grade purity were from VWR International Ltd. (Lutterworth, UK). Deuterated metaldehyde-d16 (>99 atom% deuterium) was from QMX Laboratories Ltd. (Thaxted, UK). Metaldehyde (99%) and methylamine (2 M) were from Sigma-Aldrich Ltd. (Gillingham, UK). Ultrapure water (18 MΩ ∙ cm) was used throughout and was produced from an Elga Purelab Prima water system (High Wycombe, UK).

Glassware is cleaned using a 10% Decon-90 solution (Decon Laboratories Ltd., Hove, UK), then rinsed with tap water, ultrapure water and finally methanol. Metaldehyde stock solution is prepared by dissolving 25 mg metaldehyde powder in 25 mL methanol to give a concentration of 1 g/L. The solution is kept in the dark at room temperature. Subsequent dilutions in methanol are undertaken to produce a final concentration of 50 μg/L, this solution is used to produce the aqueous calibration standards.

An internal standard stock solution (deuterated metaldehyde-d16) is prepared by dissolving 10 mg of the powder in 10 mL methanol to give a concentration of 1 g/L. Subsequent dilutions in methanol are undertaken to produce a final concentration of 50 μg/L. Each sample and calibration standard prepared in ultrapure water is spiked with internal standard to give a concentration of 1 μg/L.

A 2.5 mM methylamine + 0.05% acetic acid solution is prepared in a fume hood by adding 250 μL of acetic acid and 625 μL of 2 M methylamine to 500 mL of ultrapure water. This solution is freshly prepared every 3 days.
